title: Urban heat exposure mapping for an adaptation plan
summary: A city combined CLMS land-surface-temperature and imperviousness data with C3S climate data to target cooling interventions.
narrative:
## Challenge
Recurring heatwaves raised health risks and energy demand, but the city lacked a spatial evidence base to identify the hottest areas and prioritise greening or cool-surface interventions.

## Approach
CLMS land-surface-temperature and imperviousness products were combined with C3S climate data to build a composite urban heat-exposure index, cross-referenced with population vulnerability.

## Results
The city identified priority districts for tree planting and cool-surface pilots, and incorporated the heat-exposure mapping into its climate-adaptation plan.
